title: dataUpdate
text: dataUpdate Updates a widget's state when the data object's data is changed. dataUpdate(data) Arguments data The data object that describes the updated data. Exceptions None Return value None Description The framework calls the dataUpdate() function when the data object's data is changed. This gives the widget an opportunity to update its data-related internals. This function's argument describes what data is updated, such as value , valrep , classes , and various other types of properties. The data argument is an object with the following properties: Explain this code { "prop_id":<new or updated property value>, "html:prop_id":<new or updated property value>, "style:prop_id":<new or updated property value>, "class:class_name":<new or updated property value>, "valrep":<new or updated valrep array>, "value":<new or updated value>, } Copy There is a predefined value uniface.RESET that could be used for these property values, that is, value1, ..., valueN. The valrep array is formatted as: Explain this code [ { value: value1, representation: rep1 }, ... { value: valueN, representation: repN } ] Copy Predefined property value: uniface.RESET For all properties, beside their normal value, there is a special value uniface.RESET defined. If a property has this value in the argument of dataUpdate() , it means that the value of this property should be reset to its initial value. For example, in a dynamic server page (DSP), there is a field MY_FIELD bound with a custom widget MyCustomWidget . Its default initial value of property "style:color" can be restored by preceding it with an exclamation mark (!): Explain this code $fieldproperties(MY_FIELD) = "!style:color" Copy This will result in a call to the dataUpdate function of MyCustomWidget . Its data argument will be such as the following: Explain this code { //... "style:color":uniface.RESET, //... } Copy And the dataUpdate API function of MyCustomWidget could have an implementation, such as the following: class MyCustomWidget { //... dataUpdate(data) { //... if (data ["style:color"] === uniface.RESET) { data ["style:color"] = my_style_color_initialValue; } else { // Apply data [“style:color”] property to this widget. //... } //... } //... } Copy For more details about exclamation mark (!) and $fieldproperties , see Restoring Default Property Values in DSPs Related Links $fieldproperties Return or set the current widget properties of an instance of a field. RESET A predefined value for the properties of UX widgets, which is an instance of empty object, and is shown as an empty object {} in JavaScript debugger. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
